summary: Three net-new pure formatters (Ceil, ReserveWidth, RateUnit) added to internal/pretty with clear doc comments and no gocui/stat/SQL dependencies. pretty.Size and TestSize are untouched, the scope is confined to internal/pretty/, and the dynamic suffix logic (MB/s->GB/s divisor 1024, Mbps->Gbps divisor 1000) is correct, documented inline, and matches the nicstat Mbps convention. Tests pass and cover the overflow boundaries (threshold-1/threshold/threshold+1) plus a property walk; no critical or major issues found.

suggestions.suggestion: The largest-integer-that-fits computation uses a manual power-of-ten loop (max := 1; for ... max *= 10; max--). This is correct but slightly opaque. Consider a small named helper or a comment-anchored intermediate (e.g. maxThatFits := pow10(width) - 1) so the intent reads at a glance. Purely cosmetic; the inline comment on line 67 already explains it.
suggestions.benefit: Marginally clearer intent at the call site.

suggestions.suggestion: RateUnit promotes the unit at most once. The doc comment (lines 56-58) and the property test (pretty_test.go:135-163) correctly document that at theoretical extremes the divided value can still exceed the reserve and the field then widens deterministically (never truncates). This is acceptable per the task edge-case spec, but a single-line assertion or comment at the return site reinforcing 'no second promotion by design' would make the deliberate single-step nature explicit for future maintainers (e.g. when GB/s ranges grow). No change required.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents a future reader from mistaking the single-step promotion for an oversight.

suggestions.suggestion: The Ceil doc comment references domain context ('Verbose mode shows whole-number aggregates') inside a generic pure helper in internal/pretty. The comment is accurate and helpful, but couples a generic formatter to one caller's intent. Optional: keep the behavioral first sentence and move the verbose-mode rationale to the Task 8 call site or decisions log. Trivial.
suggestions.benefit: Keeps the generic helper's doc focused on behavior, not one consumer.

constraintVerification.functionsArePure: PASS - Ceil/ReserveWidth/RateUnit take values and return string/int, no side effects, only fmt + math.Ceil imported; no gocui/stat/SQL deps.
constraintVerification.sizeNotModified: PASS - Size (lines 9-24) and TestSize (lines 10-27) are byte-identical to HEAD~1; only additions below.
constraintVerification.scopeConfinedToPretty: PASS - git diff touches only internal/pretty/pretty.go and pretty_test.go.
constraintVerification.dynamicSuffixDiskDivisor: PASS - FamilyDisk MB/s->GB/s uses divisor 1024 (binary, consistent with Size).
constraintVerification.dynamicSuffixNetDivisor: PASS - FamilyNet Mbps->Gbps uses divisor 1000 (decimal SI), documented inline (lines 26-27, 52-53) and consistent with the nicstat print-time Mbps convention at top/stat.go:741 (Rbytes/1024/128). NOTE: the task post-completion checklist (task-01.md:160) also requires recording the chosen Mbps->Gbps divisor in 010-feat-overview-dashboard-decisions.md; that decisions file does not yet exist. This is a documentation deliverable, not a code defect, so it does not affect approval, but it should be created before the task is closed.
constraintVerification.ceilIsNetNew: PASS - internal/math/math.go contains only Min/Max(int); no ceil wrapper existed in the repo.
constraintVerification.overflowBoundaryTests: PASS - TestRateUnit_boundary covers 9999/10000/10001 for both families; TestRateUnit covers below/at/above; TestRateUnit_property walks a wide range asserting the numeric field always fits and stays padded to the reserve.
constraintVerification.lintClean: NOT_VERIFIED - golangci-lint is not installed in this environment; go vet ./internal/pretty/... is clean. Recommend running make lint in CI before merge.
